saunas.name: The Well
saunas.description: The largest spa in the Nordics. A massive playground for heat enthusiasts with distinct themed saunas and world-class Aufguss rituals.

editorial.whatToExpect: A luxurious spa experience with 10 unique pools, a cold plunge pool, and a range of invigorating sauna and Aufguss rituals.
editorial.whoItsFor: Heat enthusiasts and those seeking a high-end wellness experience.

saunas.name: Ribersborgs Kallbadhus
saunas.description: Iconic open-air bathhouse from 1898. Separate men's and women's sections with nude bathing and direct ocean access.

editorial.whatToExpect: Enjoy the warmth of the wood-burning saunas and the refreshing ocean swimming, with stunning views of the Malmö sunset.

editorial.whoItsFor: The spa is suitable for anyone seeking a relaxing and rejuvenating experience, including those looking to recover after outdoor activities.
tips: Leave digital devices behind to fully disconnect
Embrace the silence and peaceful atmosphere

saunas.name: Awana Spa
saunas.description: Bringing European-style sauna theater and Aufguss to the Las Vegas strip.

editorial.whySpecial: Awana Spa is the first of its kind, complete with personalized treatments, immersive facilities and approachable wellness offerings. It draws upon the principles of the golden ratio with rounded rooms, curved walls and highly thought out designs to promote a transformative and holistic experience.
editorial.whatToExpect: Guests can choose from a variety of treatments inspired by European and Eastern rituals with a unique social approach. A unique offering includes a Sauna-Meister-led journey that enhances your visit as they direct hot and scented steam towards you through the rhythmical movements of a towel.
highlights: Personalized treatments inspired by European and Eastern rituals
Immersive facilities and holistic wellness offerings

saunas.name: Secret Lagoon
saunas.description: Iceland's oldest swimming pool, made in 1891. A more authentic and less crowded alternative to the Blue Lagoon.
saunas.openingHours: Winter season: 1st of October to 31st of May, open daily 10:00 to 19:00 (closed 24th and 31st of December at 18:00); Summer season: 1st of June to 30th of September, open daily 10:00 to 20:00
saunas.admission: Adults (15+): €35, Children (14-): €2, Seniors (67+) and people with disabilities: €26

editorial.whySpecial: The Secret Lagoon is the oldest swimming pool in Iceland, made in 1891, and features natural geothermal hot springs that provide the water supply.
editorial.whatToExpect: A more authentic and less crowded alternative to the Blue Lagoon, with a relaxed atmosphere and the opportunity to see an active geyser.

saunas.name: Iglu Park
saunas.description: A cool village of Iglucraft saunas right on the Baltic Sea in the Noblessner district.

etiquette.towelPolicy: Wear swimwear or a towel in the shared sauna

editorial.whySpecial: Iglupark is a unique concept that combines work, leisure, and health in a well-thought-out whole, offering the opportunity to take time for yourself by visiting the iguoffice, iglucabin, and iglusauna with stunning views of the Baltic Sea.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy the endless horizon, fresh air, comfortable terrace, and time for themselves, alone or with friends and loved ones. The iglusaunas offer a view of Tallinn Bay and have a spacious front room, private terrace, and access to a swimming area.
editorial.whoItsFor: Iglupark is suitable for individuals, couples, and groups looking for a unique sauna experience with stunning sea views in Tallinn.

saunas.name: Kalma Saun
saunas.description: One of Tallinn's oldest public saunas. A truly authentic experience with massive wood-burning ovens.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Tuesday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Wednesday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Thursday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Friday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Saturday: 10 AM to 11 PM, Sunday: 10 AM to 11 PM

editorial.whySpecial: One of Tallinn's oldest public saunas with a truly authentic experience and massive wood-burning ovens.
editorial.whatToExpect: A stately sauna with a dignified atmosphere and history dating back to 1928, despite several renovations over the years.

saunas.name: Bondi Icebergs

location.address: 1 Notts Ave, Bondi Beach NSW 2026, Australia

saunas.description: The most famous pool in Australia. The sauna overlooks the pool where waves crash over the side.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Tuesday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Wednesday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Thursday: Closed, Friday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Saturday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Sunday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM

editorial.whySpecial: The Bondi Icebergs is the most famous pool in Australia, with a sauna that overlooks the pool where waves crash over the side.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy a casual bistro, two licensed bars, and dedicated function facilities at the Bondi Icebergs Club, which overlooks the sparkling blue waters of the Bondi Icebergs pool and North Bondi headland.

saunas.name: Peninsula Hot Springs
saunas.description: Award-winning natural geothermal mineral springs and day spa. The hilltop pool offers 360-degree views.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Fri 10-22, Sat-Sun 8-22

etiquette.towelPolicy: Towel, robe and locker hire included
etiquette.sessionLength: Varies by package, up to 165 minutes

editorial.whySpecial: Award-winning natural geothermal mineral springs and day spa with over 70 bathing and wellness experiences, including a hilltop pool with 360-degree views.
editorial.whatToExpect: Immerse yourself in nature as you journey through the geothermal bathing and wellness experiences. Relax in the Bath House or retreat to the adults-only Spa Dreaming Centre. Enjoy signature spa treatments and dining experiences.
editorial.whoItsFor: Families, friends, couples, and first-time visitors looking for a rejuvenating spa experience in nature.
